skill.name: tdd-workflows-tdd-green
skill.description: Implement the minimal code needed to make failing tests pass in the TDD green phase.
skill.summary: Guide for implementing minimal code to pass tests in the TDD green phase.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 105 static findings are false positives. The scanner detected markdown code block delimiters (backticks) as shell commands, hash data structures as cryptographic algorithms, and legitimate code examples as network/system activity. This is a legitimate TDD skill providing guidance on implementing minimal code to pass tests.

content.user_title: Implement Minimal Code for TDD Green Phase
content.value_statement: This skill helps Claude implement the simplest possible code to make failing tests pass, following TDD green phase principles. It guides users to write just enough code to satisfy test requirements without over-engineering.
actual_capabilities: Guide implementation of minimal code to pass failing tests
Provide TDD green phase patterns for multiple languages (JavaScript, Python, Ruby, Java, C#, Go)
Offer decision frameworks for choosing fake vs real implementations
Show progressive implementation from minimal to production-ready
Help avoid premature optimization during test-first development
Support code examples for React, Django, Express, and other frameworks
limitations: Does not write tests - only implements code to make tests pass
Does not handle refactoring for design improvements
Does not provide test creation guidance
Requires existing failing tests to work with

use_cases.title: Junior Developer Learning TDD
use_cases.description: A junior developer learning test-driven development needs guidance on what constitutes minimal implementation during the green phase. This skill helps them understand when to fake it versus implementing real logic.

use_cases.title: Codebase Modernization
use_cases.description: A team migrating legacy code to use TDD needs help implementing minimal code to satisfy new test requirements without breaking existing functionality.

use_cases.title: AI-Assisted Development
use_cases.description: Developers using Claude or Codex for AI-assisted coding need structured guidance on implementing the simplest solution that makes tests pass.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Green Phase Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tdd-workflows-tdd-green skill to implement minimal code for these failing tests: [paste test code]. Follow the green phase principles: write the simplest code that makes tests pass.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have failing tests and need minimal implementation

prompt_templates.title: Language-Specific Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Using tdd-workflows-tdd-green, implement minimal [Python/JavaScript/Ruby] code to make these tests pass: [paste tests]. Focus on the green phase approach.
prompt_templates.scenario: When working with a specific programming language

prompt_templates.title: Fake It Pattern Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the 'Fake It' TDD pattern from tdd-workflows-tdd-green to implement the minimal solution for these tests: [paste tests]. Return hard-coded values first, then evolve.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to use the fake it pattern

prompt_templates.title: Framework-Specific Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Using tdd-workflows-tdd-green, implement minimal code for [React/Django/Express] to pass these tests: [paste tests]. Show the progression from simple to production-ready.
prompt_templates.scenario: When working with a specific web framework

output_examples.input: User asks to implement code for failing tests that check user creation and email lookup
output_examples.output: The skill would guide implementation of a UserService class with in-memory storage, returning hardcoded IDs initially, then evolving to real implementation as more tests require it.

output_examples.input: User asks for help passing tests for a POST /api/tasks endpoint
output_examples.output: The skill would show progression from hardcoded response to simple in-memory state management, with notes about when to add proper architecture during refactor phase.

output_examples.input: User needs to implement a Django Product model with validation
output_examples.output: The skill would guide adding minimal validation (like price >= 0) to pass current tests, deferring complex validation and model enhancements to refactor phase.

best_practices: Write the minimum code needed to pass tests - avoid adding unrequested features
Use hard-coded returns or fake implementations when appropriate
Run tests after each small change to verify progress
Document technical debt created for later refactoring
anti_patterns: Implementing design patterns during green phase when not required by tests
Adding error handling or validation that tests do not demand
Rewriting existing working code to fix new tests
Skipping tests or modifying assertions to make them pass

faq.question: What is the TDD green phase?
faq.answer: The green phase is the second step in TDD (Red-Green-Refactor). After writing a failing test (red), you implement minimal code to make it pass (green), then refactor for improvement.

faq.question: When should I fake implementation versus real code?
faq.answer: Fake it when the implementation is complex, uncertain, or when you need to validate test structure first. Go real when the solution is obvious and simple, or when multiple tests reveal the pattern.

faq.question: Can I add error handling during green phase?
faq.answer: Only add error handling if tests explicitly require it. Otherwise, defer validation and error handling to the refactor phase to keep implementation minimal.

faq.question: How is green phase different from refactor phase?
faq.answer: Green phase focuses on making tests pass with minimal code. Refactor phase improves code structure, adds design patterns, and addresses technical debt while keeping tests green.

faq.question: What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill provides patterns for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, C#, Go, Ruby, and frameworks like React, Django, and Express.

faq.question: Does this skill write tests?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses on implementation. For writing tests, look for the complementary tdd-red skill that helps create failing tests first.
